message.content: I need to add a new migration for a user_preferences table.

Create the file `src/db/migrations/003_user_preferences.sql` with your CREATE TABLE statement. The migration runner picks up files matching `^(\d{3,})_.+\.sql$` in sorted order, so prefix with `003_`. Remember to wrap in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT so concurrent writes fail fast.
The tests are failing because `better-sqlite3` can't find its native addon.

This is a known gotcha with `better-sqlite3` — the native addon prebuild fails silently on Alpine/musl setups. Check if `better_sqlite3.node` exists in `node_modules/better-sqlite3/build`. If not, try `pnpm rebuild better-sqlite3`. The issue is usually that the prebuilt binary doesn't match the Node.js ABI. You may need `node-gyp` and Python 3 to build from source.
What command finds all observations with a specific tag?

Tags are stored as a JSON array in the `tags` column. To find observations with a specific tag via raw SQL: `SELECT * FROM observations WHERE json_each(tags) MATCH '$.value = "my-tag"'` — or via the CLI: `somtum search my-tag`. The BM25 retriever will pick up the tag text since it indexes the body and title fields.
I ran `pnpm reindex` and it worked! All 42 embeddings populated.

Great. The `reindex` command calls `embedMissing`, which walks all observations without a vector and embeds them in batches of 16. Now hybrid and embeddings strategies will work. Make sure `retrieval.embeddings.enabled` is `true` in your config, otherwise the factory will fall back to BM25.
